description:
<title>Temporarily switch shell to UTC & C.UTF-8 and generate verification log</title>

  <details>
    You are a web-developer who just logged into a freshly-provisioned Linux host.  
    For a quick diagnostic you must verify that the current interactive shell is using:

      • the UTC time-zone  
      • the C.UTF-8 locale  

    Your goal is to:

      1. Change *only* the environment of the current shell (no system-wide files, no sudo)
         so that the following command reports UTC and C.UTF-8 respectively:

             date +"%Z"
             printf "%s\n" "$LC_ALL"

      2. Immediately create the log file  
         /home/user/time_locale_check.log  
         whose contents must be *exactly* (including capitalisation, the equal signs,
         and the final line-feed character):

             TIMEZONE=UTC
             LOCALE=C.UTF-8

         • The file must contain **two lines** and nothing else.  
         • Line-1 must end with a single “\n”; line-2 must also end with a single “\n”.  
         • No leading or trailing spaces on either line.

    Important notes for automated checking:

      • The agent will be evaluated only on the presence and exact byte-for-byte contents
        of /home/user/time_locale_check.log.  
      • The tester will not require the variables to persist after the session—just that
        the log file is produced while the shell is in the requested state.  

    Use whatever single or composite shell command(s) you like, but remember:
    the log file must match the two-line specification above verbatim.
  </details>
name: task_000000_ab4f6ec0
